description: CER Chapter Defense Rules — per-chapter guard configuration for DeerFlow Writer Agent
source_assets: CER_05_TABLES_表格体系与证据脉络分析.md

defense_points.name: No new information introduced
defense_points.description: Summary must not introduce evidence or data not present in subsequent chapters

defense_points.name: Contains all required elements
defense_points.description: Must include: device name, intended purpose, evaluation type, key findings, overall conclusion

defense_points.name: Length within guideline
defense_points.description: Summary should be 200-800 words; excessive length indicates scope creep

defense_points.name: Matches §5 Conclusions
defense_points.description: Summary conclusion statements must be consistent with §5, not stronger or weaker

chapters.writer_guard_prompt: You are writing §1 Summary of a Clinical Evaluation Report. RULES: (1) NEVER introduce new evidence or data here — only summarize what follows; (2) Include: device name, intended purpose, evaluation type, key evidence sources, and overall conclusion; (3) Keep length between 200-800 words; (4) Ensure conclusion strength matches §5 exactly — do not strengthen or weaken; (5) Use present tense; (6) No tables in this section.

defense_points.name: Scope clearly defined
defense_points.description: Must define the scope of the clinical evaluation including device identification and evaluation objectives

defense_points.name: Regulatory context stated
defense_points.description: Must reference applicable MDR articles and relevant harmonized standards

chapters.writer_guard_prompt: You are writing §0 Scope & Introduction. RULES: (1) Define the scope and objectives of the clinical evaluation; (2) Identify the device name, model, and manufacturer; (3) Reference applicable regulations (MDR Art 61) and standards; (4) State the evaluation approach (literature-only / clinical investigation / combined); (5) Keep concise.

defense_points.name: Device identity anchor complete
defense_points.description: Must define: device name, model/variant, EMDN code, MDR classification (Class + Rule), intended purpose

defense_points.name: All variants documented
defense_points.description: If multiple models/variants exist, each must be described with functional differences

defense_points.name: Principle of operation stated
defense_points.description: Working mechanism must be described in sufficient detail for technical understanding

defense_points.name: Marketing history included
defense_points.description: Must include: countries of authorization, sales data if available, vigilance summary if available

defense_points.name: Previous generations described
defense_points.description: Must describe predecessor devices and design evolution rationale

defense_points.name: Contraindications and warnings listed
defense_points.description: Must include contraindications, warnings, precautions, and intended user/environment

defense_points.name: Table/Figure references consistent
defense_points.description: All Table/Figure references in §2 must have corresponding tables in this section or Annex

chapters.writer_guard_prompt: You are writing §2 Scope / Device Description. RULES: (1) Device identity MUST include: name, model/variant, EMDN code, MDR Class+Rule, intended purpose — this is NON-NEGOTIABLE; (2) If multiple variants exist, describe EACH with functional differences; (3) Include: contraindications, warnings, precautions, intended users, intended environment; (4) Describe previous generations and design evolution; (5) Include marketing history (authorized countries, sales data if available); (6) Use present tense; (7) Passive voice acceptable for technical descriptions; (8) Sentence length 22-32 words average; (9) Every Table/Figure must be referenced in text.

defense_points.name: Intended purpose explicitly stated
defense_points.description: Must clearly state the intended purpose, indications, contra-indications, target population, and intended users

defense_points.name: Intended purpose consistent with IFU
defense_points.description: Intended purpose in CER must match the IFU/labeling exactly

chapters.writer_guard_prompt: You are writing §2 Intended Purpose & Indications. RULES: (1) EXPLICITLY state intended medical indication and purpose; (2) List ALL indications and contra-indications; (3) Define target population (age, condition, anatomical location); (4) Define intended users (clinical specialty, training level); (5) Ensure 100% consistency with IFU — any deviation is a Blocker defect.

defense_points.name: Three core questions answered
defense_points.description: Must answer Q1 (Gold Standard), Q2 (Safety risk differential), Q3 (Unmet need vs optimization)

defense_points.name: Disease epidemiology present
defense_points.description: Must include: disease prevalence, incidence, natural history, disease burden

defense_points.name: Alternative treatments described
defense_points.description: Must describe all alternative treatments: pharmacological, surgical, interventional, other devices

defense_points.name: Clinical practice guidelines cited
defense_points.description: Must cite relevant clinical guidelines with publication year and issuing body

defense_points.name: Safety and Performance Endpoints defined
defense_points.description: Must define SOTA benchmark values for safety and performance endpoints in tabular format

defense_points.name: Potential hazards identified
defense_points.description: Must list known risks and complications of the disease and existing treatments

defense_points.name: Search strategy documented (if standalone)
defense_points.description: If SOTA search strategy is in §3, must include: databases, search terms, inclusion/exclusion criteria, dates

defense_points.name: SOTA → §4 evidence chain established
defense_points.description: SOTA conclusions must explicitly set up the evidentiary questions that §4 will answer

defense_points.name: Evaluation type explicitly stated
defense_points.description: Must clearly state: literature-only, clinical investigation, or combined approach

defense_points.name: Justification for evaluation type
defense_points.description: Must justify why this evaluation type is appropriate for the device risk class and novelty

defense_points.name: Reference to Clinical Evaluation Plan
defense_points.description: Must reference the Clinical Evaluation Plan that defines this approach

required_evidence: Published safety/performance data for SOTA benchmark values
chapters.writer_guard_prompt:
You are writing §3 Clinical Background / SOTA. CRITICAL RULES: (1) MUST answer three core questions before writing: Q1=What is the gold standard? Q2=What is the safety risk differential vs gold standard? Q3=Is this unmet need or optimization?; (2) MUST include Safety and Performance Endpoints table with SOTA benchmark values — this is NON-NEGOTIABLE; (3) Disease epidemiology MUST include prevalence/incidence with citations; (4) Alternative treatments MUST cover all categories: pharmacological, surgical, interventional, other devices; (5) Clinical guidelines MUST be cited with year and issuing body; (6) Known hazards MUST be listed; (7) SOTA conclusions MUST explicitly frame the evidentiary questions for §4; (8) Use present tense for disease description, past tense for study history; (9) Paragraph length 25-40 words.

---

You are writing §4.1 Type of Evaluation. RULES: (1) EXPLICITLY state the evaluation type: literature-only / clinical investigation / combined; (2) JUSTIFY why this type is appropriate for the device's risk class and novelty; (3) REFERENCE the Clinical Evaluation Plan; (4) If clinical investigation path: mention study design, population, endpoints; (5) If literature-only path: explain why sufficient published evidence exists; (6) Keep concise — this is a declarative section.

defense_points.name: Equivalence decision documented
defense_points.description: Must explicitly state whether equivalence is claimed or not; if not, must explain why

defense_points.name: Three-dimension comparison table present
defense_points.description: If equivalence claimed: MUST have Technical + Biological + Clinical comparison table per MDR Art 61

defense_points.name: Every difference justified
defense_points.description: Each 'Different' or 'Similar' entry must have impact assessment: 'does not affect safety/performance because...'

defense_points.name: Equivalent device is EU-authorized
defense_points.description: The claimed equivalent device must have CE mark or equivalent authorization in target market

defense_points.name: Intended purpose identical
defense_points.description: Intended purpose must be identical (not just similar) between subject and equivalent device

defense_points.name: Reference to §2 device specs
defense_points.description: Must reference §2 device description parameters in the comparison (≥3 references recommended)

defense_points.name: Similar devices table (if no equivalence)
defense_points.description: If equivalence NOT claimed: must include similar devices comparison table for market positioning

chapters.writer_guard_prompt: You are writing §4.2 Demonstration of Equivalence. CRITICAL RULES: (1) FIRST sentence must declare: 'Equivalence is [claimed / not claimed]'; (2) If CLAIMED: MUST include three-dimension comparison table (Technical / Biological / Clinical) per MDR Art 61; (3) If CLAIMED: intended purpose MUST be IDENTICAL — this is NON-NEGOTIABLE; (4) Every 'Different' or 'Similar' entry MUST have impact justification ending with 'does not affect clinical safety and performance because...'; (5) MUST reference §2 device specs ≥3 times; (6) Equivalent device MUST be EU-authorized (CE marked); (7) If NOT claimed: MUST include Similar Devices table for market positioning; (8) Use standard wording: 'Substantially equivalent' / 'Equivalent with minor differences' / 'Not equivalent'; (9) Reference MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4 and MDCG 2020-5 in opening.

defense_points.name: Standards conformance documented
defense_points.description: Must list all applicable harmonized standards with conformance status and evidence reference

defense_points.name: Pre-clinical evaluation summary present
defense_points.description: Must include: bench testing, biocompatibility (ISO 10993), animal studies, usability testing

defense_points.name: Clinical investigation data complete
defense_points.description: If CI exists: must include design, population, endpoints, results, statistical analysis

defense_points.name: Literature search strategy complete
defense_points.description: Must include: databases, search terms, inclusion/exclusion, screening process, dates, executors

defense_points.name: Literature appraisal performed
defense_points.description: Must include quality assessment using recognized tools (Cochrane RoB, NOS, etc.)

defense_points.name: Vigilance data from ≥2 databases
defense_points.description: Must search MHRA + BfArM + FDA MAUDE (minimum: 2 databases); include search date and terms

defense_points.name: GSPR analysis covers all applicable requirements
defense_points.description: Must address: GSPR 1 (safety/performance), GSPR 6 (benefit-risk), GSPR 8 (side-effects) minimum

defense_points.name: Each GSPR has ≥2 evidence types
defense_points.description: Each GSPR analysis must cite at least 2 types of evidence (standard, pre-clinical, clinical, literature, vigilance)

defense_points.name: Five-paragraph GSPR structure followed
defense_points.description: Each GSPR: Requirement Restatement → Evidence Sources → Evidence Summary → Analysis → Conformity Conclusion

defense_points.name: Benefit-risk analysis complete
defense_points.description: Must include: clinical benefits (quantified), clinical risks (quantified), risk mitigation, overall conclusion

defense_points.name: Quantitative analysis where possible
defense_points.description: Must use quantitative data where available; qualitative only when quantitative impossible

defense_points.name: Data consistency across subsections
defense_points.description: Numbers cited in §4.3, §4.4, §4.5, §4.6, §4.7 must be mutually consistent

required_evidence: Vigilance database search results (MHRA, BfArM, FDA)
chapters.writer_guard_prompt: You are writing §4 Evidence Analysis (the largest and most critical section). ABSOLUTE RULES: (1) GSPR analysis MUST cover GSPR 1, 6, 8 minimum; (2) EACH GSPR MUST cite ≥2 evidence types (standard, pre-clinical, clinical, literature, vigilance); (3) EACH GSPR MUST follow 5-paragraph structure: Requirement → Sources → Summary → Analysis → Conclusion; (4) Benefit-risk analysis MUST quantify benefits AND risks with rates/percentages; (5) Vigilance data MUST cover ≥2 databases (MHRA+BfArM+FDA minimum); (6) Literature search MUST document: databases, terms, inclusion/exclusion, dates, executors; (7) ALL numerical data MUST be consistent across §4 subsections — cross-check before finalizing; (8) Standards conformance table MUST list all applicable harmonized standards; (9) Use past tense for study results, present tense for analysis conclusions; (10) Sentence length 25-30 words for analysis paragraphs.

defense_points.name: Benefits quantified
defense_points.description: Must quantify clinical benefits with appropriate metrics and confidence intervals

defense_points.name: Risks quantified
defense_points.description: Must quantify clinical risks including probability and severity assessments

defense_points.name: Risk mitigation measures documented
defense_points.description: Must describe risk control measures and residual risk assessment

defense_points.name: Overall benefit-risk conclusion
defense_points.description: Must provide explicit conclusion on benefit-risk acceptability

chapters.writer_guard_prompt: You are writing §6 Benefit-Risk Analysis. CRITICAL RULES: (1) QUANTIFY all benefits and risks with rates/percentages where possible; (2) Document ALL risk control measures from ISO 14971; (3) Assess RESIDUAL risk after controls; (4) Provide EXPLICIT overall benefit-risk conclusion using 'acceptable when weighed against the benefits' phrasing; (5) Reference MDR Annex I GSPR requirements.

defense_points.name: Limitations statement present
defense_points.description: Must include limitations declaration (industry standard wording acceptable)

defense_points.name: Overall conformity conclusion
defense_points.description: Must state that device meets relevant GSPRs based on evaluated evidence

defense_points.name: Safety conclusion
defense_points.description: Must explicitly state safety conclusion for claimed use

defense_points.name: Performance conclusion
defense_points.description: Must explicitly state performance conclusion for claimed use

defense_points.name: Benefit-risk conclusion
defense_points.description: Must explicitly state benefit-risk acceptability

defense_points.name: Next evaluation plan
defense_points.description: Must include next evaluation date or trigger event

defense_points.name: Conclusion strength matches evidence
defense_points.description: Wording strength must match evidence strength (see strength matching table)

defense_points.name: Each conclusion traceable to §4
defense_points.description: Every conclusion statement must have corresponding evidence in §4

defense_points.name: No new evidence introduced
defense_points.description: Conclusions must not introduce evidence not presented in §4

defense_points.name: Conciseness enforced
defense_points.description: Each sentence = one judgment; no clause stacking; 150-300 words total

chapters.writer_guard_prompt: You are writing §5 Conclusions. CRITICAL RULES: (1) MUST contain EXACTLY these 6 elements: Limitations + Overall Conformity + Safety + Performance + Benefit-Risk + Next Evaluation; (2) EACH conclusion MUST be traceable to §4 evidence — if you cannot find the evidence, DO NOT write the conclusion; (3) NEVER introduce new evidence here; (4) Conclusion strength MUST match evidence: strong evidence → 'confirms/demonstrates', moderate → 'indicates/supports', weak → 'is consistent with'; (5) EACH sentence = ONE judgment only — no clause stacking; (6) Use present tense; (7) Keep 150-300 words total; (8) Industry standard limitation wording is acceptable: 'The limitations...unpublished documents about negative result cannot be examined.'; (9) Benefit-risk conclusion MUST use 'acceptable when weighed against the benefits' phrasing.

defense_points.name: Literature list complete
defense_points.description: All literature cited in §4.4/§4.5 must have full bibliographic entries in Annex

defense_points.name: Search strategy details annexed
defense_points.description: Complete search strategies (screenshots or full text) should be in Annex

defense_points.name: Equivalence table fully detailed
defense_points.description: If §4.2 has summary equivalence table, Annex should have full detailed version

defense_points.name: Standards list complete
defense_points.description: All standards referenced in §4.3.1 must be listed with full designation

defense_points.name: Vigilance search records preserved
defense_points.description: Screenshots or records of vigilance database searches should be in Annex

defense_points.name: Annex references in main text
defense_points.description: Every Annex must be referenced at least once in the main text

defense_points.name: No orphan annexes
defense_points.description: No Annex should exist without being referenced in the main text

required_evidence: Complete literature list with full bibliographic details
chapters.writer_guard_prompt: You are compiling Annexes. RULES: (1) Literature List: MUST include ALL literature cited in §4.4/§4.5 with full bibliographic details; (2) Search Strategy Annex: include screenshots or full text of database searches; (3) Equivalence Table Annex: if §4.2 has summary, Annex has FULL detailed version; (4) Standards List: all standards from §4.3.1 with complete designations; (5) Vigilance Records: screenshots of database searches; (6) CRITICAL: every Annex MUST be referenced ≥1 time in main text — orphan annexes are NOT allowed; (7) Number Annexes sequentially (Annex 1, Annex 2...); (8) Each Annex must have a title page.

defense_points.name: Next evaluation date specified
defense_points.description: Must specify date or trigger condition for next CER update

defense_points.name: Evaluator qualifications documented
defense_points.description: Must include names, titles, and relevant qualifications of evaluators

defense_points.name: Declaration of interest included
defense_points.description: Must include conflict of interest declarations

defense_points.name: Dates and signatures complete
defense_points.description: Must include approval dates and signatures

chapters.writer_guard_prompt: You are writing §6-§9 Administrative Sections. RULES: (1) §6 Next Evaluation: specify date or trigger condition; (2) §7 Qualification: list evaluator names, titles, qualifications; (3) §8 Declaration: include conflict of interest statement; (4) §9 Signatures: include approval dates and signature placeholders; (5) These sections are template-driven — ensure no template placeholders remain (TM-001 risk); (6) Keep minimal — administrative only, no technical content.

cross_chapter_validation_rules.name: §2 → §4.2 device spec consistency
cross_chapter_validation_rules.description: All device specifications in §4.2 equivalence table must match §2 device description

cross_chapter_validation_rules.name: §3 SOTA → §4.7 analysis benchmark consistency
cross_chapter_validation_rules.description: SOTA benchmark values in §3.9 must match values cited in §4.7 analysis

cross_chapter_validation_rules.name: §4 → §5 conclusion traceability
cross_chapter_validation_rules.description: Every statement in §5 must have supporting evidence in §4

cross_chapter_validation_rules.name: §4.4 literature count → Annex literature list count
cross_chapter_validation_rules.description: Number of included literature in §4.4 must equal number of entries in Annex literature list

cross_chapter_validation_rules.name: §1 Summary → §5 Conclusions wording consistency
cross_chapter_validation_rules.description: Summary and Conclusions must use identical conclusion strength and statements

cross_chapter_validation_rules.name: Table numbering continuity
cross_chapter_validation_rules.description: All Table references in text must have corresponding tables; no gaps in numbering
